title: Police News Release - May 12th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - May 12th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 12, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours one individual was arrested on the strength of their Warrant for Arrest. Released with a future court date of June 8
th
, 2026.
Breach of the Peace / Protective Detention and Care of Intoxicated Persons Act
Over the last 24 hours, police attended 4 separate incidents in which 3 individuals were arrested and lodged until sober.
Failure to Comply Release Order
On May 11
th
at 10:06 p.m police were requested to remove a 52 year old male from a hotel in the 1900 block of Queens Avenue. The male was found to be breaching Release Orders. He was arrested and held in detention cells for court in the morning.
Theft Under $5000
On April 15
th
, a bicycle was reported stolen from outside an apartment building in the 2100 block of Brandon Avenue. Security footage later identified two suspects: a 31-year-old man and a 37-year-old woman.
On May 11
th
, both individuals were arrested in connection with the theft. They were subsequently released on Appearance Notices and are scheduled to appear in court at a later date. The male is set to appear on July 16
th
, while the female’s court date is July 13
th
.

title: Police News Release - May 11th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - May 11th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 11, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Protective Detention and Care of Intoxicated Persons Act
Over the last 24 hours, police attended an incident in which one individual was arrested and held in custody until sober.
Arrest Warrant
In the past 24 hours two individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. One was released via Appearance Notice with a future court date, and one was held in custody for court in the morning on May 11
th
, 2026.
Break & Enter
BPS has arrested three suspects in connection with a break and enter reported at a multi-unit property on McDiarmid Drive.
On May 8
th
, 2026, police received a report from a property manager regarding a break and enter that occurred on May 7
th
at approximately 10:10 p.m. During the incident, a secure interior door was damaged, a surveillance device was stolen, and attempts were made to gain access to the change slots of laundry machines within the building. Police reviewed video surveillance footage and identified the three individuals involved, some of whom were bound by conditions prohibiting communication with one another.
On May 10
th
, while on patrol, a BPS officer observed one of the suspects outside a hotel in the 3500 block of Victoria Avenue. Upon noticing the officer, the female suspect quickly entered the building. Police utilized drone footage to assist in locating the suspect, along with a second suspect who was found hiding in a nearby yard. The two women, aged 26 and 29, were arrested without further incident.
At approximately 11:45 p.m., officers attended a residence in the 1200 block of 1st Street where they located and arrested the third suspect, a 36-year-old male.
The 29-year-old female has been charged with Break, Enter and Commit – Theft Under, Disguise with Intent, Breach of Release Order, Breach of Undertaking, and Possession of Break-In Instruments.
The 36-year-old male has been charged with Failure to Comply with an Undertaking x2 and Break and Enter with Intent.
The 26-year-old female has been charged with Break and Enter with Intent. She was released on an undertaking with numerous conditions and is scheduled to appear in court on July 13
th
, 2026.
The 36-year-old male and the 29-year-old female were held in BPS custody for court on the morning of May 11
th
, 2026.
J. Reichert
Recruitment & Communications Specialist
Crime Stoppers Coordinator
Community Support

title: Police News Release - May 8th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - May 8th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 08, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours three individuals from separate incidents were arrested due to Breach of the Peace. They were each brought to BPS detention cells to prevent the continuation of behaviour and will be released once sober/calm.
Fraud Under $5000
On May 7
th
, a 48-year-old male was arrested and charged with Fraud Under $5,000 after posting an online advertisement offering sea can containers for sale. Earlier that morning, the suspect met with a buyer in the 800 block of Victoria Avenue and accepted $1,500 in cash for the two containers that were to be picked up at 5:00 p.m. that day.
It was later determined that the sea cans belonged to a business located in the 300 block of Pacific Avenue and were not for sale. Investigators were able to positively identify the suspect after the buyers provided a photograph showing the male holding the cash. Police subsequently located the suspect at a hotel in the 100 block of 5
th
Street at approximately 4:22 p.m., where he was arrested. The accused was later released on an Undertaking and is scheduled to appear in court on July 2
nd
, 2026.
Theft Under $5000
On March 12
th
a 23-year-old female suspect was caught on surveillance video stealing over $300 of clothing from a store in Corral Centre. The woman was identified and on May 7
th
she was arrested, charged and later released
on an Undertaking and is scheduled to appear in court on July 9
th
, 2026.
Arrest Warrant
On May 7
th
a 30-year-old male was located at his home on Cornell Bay and arrested on the strength of his Warrant for Arrest (Failure to Comply Release Order). The male was later released via Appearance Notice with a future court date of June 8
th
, 2026.
Possession of a Weapon
On May 8
th
at approximately 2:41 p.m., police responded to a call in the 300 block of 16th Street following reports of an intoxicated male yelling and causing a disturbance. Upon arrival, officers observed the male answer the door while holding a machete and yelling at police. Officers issued commands, which the 20-year-old male complied with, and he was subsequently arrested and taken into custody at BPS detention cells. The accused will be released once sober on an Undertaking and is scheduled to appear in court on July 20
th
, 2026.
Motor Vehicle Collision
On May 8
th
at 3:24 a.m. BPS was dispatched to a motor vehicle accident ay Highway #1 and 18
th
Street. A car had rearended a semi and the vehicle was trapped underneath the trailer. The 25-year-old male driver of the car was able to free himself and he was transported by ambulance to Brandon Regional Health Centre suffering what appeared to be only minor injuries.
BPS members remained on scene until approximately 5:20 a.m. to assist in clearing the wreckage off the roadway. Tow trucks will tend to the scene in daylight to retrieve the semi-trailer from the side of the road.
J. Reichert

title: Police News Release- May 7th, 2026
content:
Police News Release- May 7th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 07, 2026
Police Media Releases
Obtain Sexual Services for Consideration
On 2026-05-06, it was reported to police that, between the dates of January 2025 and April 2026, a 76-year-old man was buying items for a female in exchange for sexual services. This male has since been arrested and released to attend Court in June 2026.
Extortion, Distribute Child Porn, Possess Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material
On 2026-01-19, it was reported to police that a female youth had sent intimate images of herself to a male. This 16-year-old male was now extorting the female requesting more photos or he would release the photo she had sent him. The accused proceeded to share the photo and on 2026-05-06 he was arrested. The accused will have court in July 2026 facing the above noted charges.
Intoxicated Youth/Breach of Peace
On 2026-05-07 at 0037 hrs, Police were requested to a home on Westaway Bay for a highly intoxicated youth causing a disturbance within a residence. The youth was arrested for her own safety and the safety of others in the home. She will be released in the morning to an adult once sober.
Cst. M. Jefferies

title: City Council Accepting Applications to the Brandon Police Board
content:
City Council Accepting Applications to the Brandon Police Board
By
City of Brandon
-
May 04, 2026
News & Notices
Council and Committees
Police Media Releases
Brandon, MB  - City Council will be considering the following citizen appointments for a four-year term:
Brandon Police Board - 5 positions starting November 2026
Eligibility requirements
is at least 18 years of age;
is a Canadian citizen or a permanent resident; and
resides or is employed in, or has a business interest in, the municipality in question or another area in which the police service provides policing services.
The following persons may not be appointed to a municipal police board:
a judge of the Court of King's Bench or The Court of Appeal;
a provincial judge or justice of the peace;
a member of the Legislative Assembly of Manitoba or of the Senate or House of Commons of Canada; or
a current or former member of the police service for which the police board is responsible.
Applications must be accompanied by:
a Certified Criminal Record Check based on the submission of fingerprints, and;
a Child Abuse Registry Self Check Application for the province of Manitoba
Associated costs will be reimbursed to successful appointees.
NOTE:
Appointees must be willing and able to commit the time necessary to properly fulfill their responsibilities on the Board, including preparing for, attending, and participating at all scheduled Board meetings. The Brandon Police Board meets at City Hall a minimum of six times per year on Fridays, 11:45 a.m. - 3 p.m.
Application forms are available at www.brandon.ca\ or can be picked up at and returned to the Legislative Services Office, Second Floor, City Hall, 410 - 9th Street, NO LATER THAN 4:00 pm on JUNE 1, 2026.
Further information on the duties and activities of the Boards and Committees of City Council can be obtained at www.brandon.ca, by calling 204-729-2296 or emailing cityclerk@brandon.ca.
All applications shall be considered by City Council in its selection process, and such appointments shall be final.
Public Notice: Appointments to Brandon Police Board

title: Police News Release - May 5th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - May 5th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 05, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours three individuals were arrested on the strength of their Brandon Police Service (BPS) Warrants for Arrest. One was held for court in the morning while the two others were released via Appearance Notice with future court dates.
Criminal Harassment x2 / Breach of Peace Bond
Between August 2025 and May 2026, a 49-year-old female repeatedly harassed her former partner and was found to be in breach of the conditions of her Peace Bond.
On May 4
th
, 2026, the female was located, arrested, and held in custody for a court appearance the following morning. The female was also issued a cease-and-desist letter by the BPS, directing her to stop contacting police for non-emergency matters, as she has frequently and routinely tied up police resources with non-urgent issues.
Theft Under $5000 / Breach of Undertaking x2
On May 4
th
at approximately 3:32 p.m., police received a call from a business located in the 1500 block of 18th Street (Shoppers Mall) reporting that a male had just stolen a hockey stick valued at approximately $300. Surveillance footage was available, and the license plate of the suspect vehicle was obtained. Police located the male in the vehicle shortly thereafter; however, the stolen hockey stick was not in his possession. The male stated that he had discarded the item near a dumpster. The male was also the subject of a separate investigation, as he had been communicating with his ex-partner and had also attended her residence - both actions being in violation of the conditions of his Undertaking. He was arrested and held in custody at BPS for a court appearance the following morning on May 5
th
, 2026.
Structure Fire
On May 4
th
, 2026, at 11:12 p.m., police responded to a 911 call and assisted Brandon Fire & Emergency Services (BFES) by attending to a fire in the 300 block of Victoria Avenue. The fire is believed to have originated in a greenhouse and spread to an adjacent deck before being contained and extinguished by BFES. No injuries were reported, and the fire is not considered suspicious.
Protective Detention & Care of Intoxicated Persons Act (PDCIPA)
On May 4
th
at 11:00 p.m. police responded to a report of an intoxicated male stumbling around someone’s yard in the 800 block of 11
th
Street. Police attended and arrested a highly intoxicated 38-year-old male. He was brought to BPS detention cells and will be released once sober.
Breach of Probation x2
On May 5
th
at 12:35 a.m. police came across a known to police 17-year-old female in the 800 block of Rosser Avenue. The female was breaching her curfew and a do not attend probation clause. She was arrested, brought to BPS, and will attend court in the morning on May 5
th
, 2026.
Missing Child
On the evening of May 4
th
, 2026, at 6:19 p.m., police received a report from a mother that her seven-year-old non-verbal autistic daughter was missing. The child had been playing in the yard with her siblings but did not return inside with them.
BPS immediately responded, deploying on-duty platoon members, cadets, as well as RPAS (drone) and K9 units. Family, friends, and members of the community also assisted in the search efforts.
The child was safely located approximately one hour later. The child had wandered approximately eight blocks to a store, where she was found looking at children’s books. BPS would like to sincerely thank the community for their immediate concern and assistance in sharing information and helping with the search.
J. Reichert
Recruitment & Communications Specialist
Crime Stoppers Coordinator
Community Support

title: Police News Release- May 2nd, 2026
content:
Police News Release-May 2nd, 2026
Back to News Search
Subscribe
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 02, 2026
Police Media Releases
Assault with a Weapon
On May 1, 2026, shortly after 1:00 pm, police responded to a report of an individual who had been sprayed with bear mase in the 800 block of Rosser Ave.  The victim received medical treatment and was released.
Police located and arrested the 16-year-old male suspect nearby.  He was to abide by a court order not to possess any weapons.  He was lodged to appear in court today on charges of Assault with a Weapon, Possess Weapon Dangerous, Carry Concealed Weapon and Fail to Comply with Probation X2.
Breach of Undertaking
On May 2, 2026, shortly after 3:00 am, police responded to a report of a male and female screaming at each other at a residence. The 20-year-old male was to abide by conditions not to attend the address.  He was arrested and lodged to be released once sober.  He will appear in court on a later date.
Breach of the Peace / IPDA
Over the past 24 hours 6 individuals were arrested during separate incidents and lodged until sober due to their behavior stemming from drugs or alcohol consumption.
Arrest Warrants
Over the past 24 hours 3 individuals were arrested on outstanding warrants.

title: Police News Release - May 1st, 2026
content:
Police News Release - May 1st, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
May 01, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Protective Detention & Care of Intoxicated Persons Act (PDCIPA)
In the past 24 hours two individuals from separate incidents were arrested due to intoxication levels. They were held in Brandon Police Service (BPS) detention cells to be released once sober.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours five individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. One was additionally charged with Resist Arrest. Three were later released with future court dates and two were held in BPS detention cells for court in the morning on May 1
st
, 2026.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours two individuals from separate incidents were arrested due to Breach of the Peace. They were each brought to BPS detention cells to prevent the continuation of behaviour and will be released once sober/calm.
Assault & Firearm Charges
On March 25th, it was reported to an officer that on March 20th at approximately 11:00 p.m., the victim (18-year-old male) and a couple of friends attended a restaurant located on the 2600 block of Victoria Avenue. While there, the accused (16-year-old male) and one other male, began bothering them. When the victim and friends left the restaurant, the accused followed them into the parking lot. The accused then took a bag from another individual and brandished what appeared to be a handgun at them.
An investigation began and at approximately 1:36 p.m. on April 30th, the accused, a 16-year-old male youth, attended BPS and turned himself in. He was held in BPS detention cells and is scheduled to appear in court on the morning of May 1st, 2026. Charges include Assault with a Weapon, Carry a Concealed Weapon, Possession of a Weapon, and Possession of a Firearm during the Commission of an Offence.
Disobey Court Order x3 / Obstruct Justice
On April 29th, a 42-year-old female reported that her ex-boyfriend, a 37-year-old male, had breached court no contact orders. The complainant stated that the accused sent her an e-transfer containing a message in the memo field. Additionally, on April 28th, 2026, the accused was observed on surveillance footage dropping off a three-page letter in an apparent attempt to persuade her to withdraw charges against him.
On April 30th, the male was arrested and charged. He was subsequently released on an Appearance Notice with a court date of July 9th, 2026.
Failure to Comply Release Order
On April 30
th
at 11:16 p.m. police conducted a curfew check on a 51-year-old male who is to abide by a 9pm – 7am curfew. He was not at home. Approximately ten minutes later the male arrived home, and police were still in the area. He was arrested and held
in BPS detention cells for court in the morning on May 1
st
, 2026.

title: April 29th, 2026, Brandon Police Service Special News Release
content:
April 29th, 2026, Brandon Police Service Special News Release
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 29, 2026
Police Media Releases
Public Engagement
On March 27, 2026, Brandon Police Service Major Crime Section members began an investigation into a break and enter where multiple firearms were stolen along with other property from a residence in the 400 block of 22
nd
Street in Brandon. As a result, five of the stolen firearms were recovered and four suspects were identified and arrested on March 27 and 28. MCS members have continued the investigation into the remaining stolen firearms and on April 28, MCS with the assistance of PDSU, Patrol and Community Support members executed a Search Warrant in the 2300 block of Brandon Avenue.
A suspect was arrested and during the search of his person and the residence MCS members located a Sawed-Off 12 Gauge Shotgun, confirmed to be stolen from the break and enter on 22
nd
Street along with ammunition. An additional loaded, Sawed-Off .22 Caliber Rifle was also located within the residence along with packaging material, scales, methamphetamines and other weapons.
Johnny Isaac CAMPBELL, 23, of Brandon has been charged with:
Sec. 91(2) C.C. – Unauthorized Possession of Prohibited Weapon x 2
Sec. 92 C.C. - Possession of Firearm Knowing its Possession is Unauthorized x 2
Sec. 95 C.C. – Possession of Prohibited Firearm with Ammunition x 2
Sec. 96 C.C. – Possession of Firearm Obtained by Crime
Sec. 86(1) C.C. – Careless Storage of Firearm / Ammunition x 4
Sec. 88 C.C. – Possession of Weapon x 2
Sec. 90 C.C. – Carrying Concealed Weapon x 2
Sec. 117.01(1) C.C. – Possession of Firearm Contrary to Prohibition Order x 8
Sec. 733.1(1) C.C. – Fail to Comply with Probation Order x 4
Sec. 145(5) C.C - Fail to Comply with Release Order x 2
Sec. 354(1) C.C. – Possession of Property Obtained by Crime
Sec. 354(1) C.C. – Possession of Proceeds of Crime
Sec. 5(2) CDSA – Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking (Methamphetamine)
Campbell was remanded into custody.
Jordan Lee BRANDON, 31, of Brandon is wanted by Brandon Police Service and a Warrant has been issued for his arrest. BRANDON has been charged with:
Sec. 98(1) C.C. - Break and Enter to Steal Firearm
Sec. 348(1)(b) C.C. - Break and Enter and Commit Theft over $5000
Sec. 91(1) C.C. - Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm x 9
Sec. 86(1) C.C. - Careless Storage of a Firearm x 5
Sec. 354(1) C.C. – Possession of Property Obtained by Crime
This investigation remains ongoing and if anyone has information about the Break and Enter, or whereabouts of Jordan Lee BRANDON, they are asked to please call Brandon Police Service at 204-729-2345 or Brandon Crimestoppers at 204-727-TIPS (8477).

By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 28, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours five individuals were arrested due to Breach of the Peace. They were each brought to Brandon Police Service (BPS) detention cells to prevent the continuation of behaviour and will be released once sober/calm.
Protective Detention & Care of Intoxicated Persons Act (PDCIPA)
In the past 24 hours three individuals from separate incidents were arrested due to intoxication levels. They were held in BPS detention cells to be released once sober.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours three individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. Two were released via Appearance Notices with future court dates while one was held for court in the morning on April 28
th
, 2026.
Robbery with Violence
On April 27
th
at 8:12 p.m., police received a report from a witness stating that several girls had “jumped” and assaulted another female at the mall located in the 1500 block of 18
th
Street. Investigation determined that the victim, a 15-year-old female, had been lured to the mall under the pretense of meeting other girls to “hang out.” Upon arrival, she was assaulted by multiple suspects, who kicked and punched her before stealing her shoes. The investigation identified two suspects: a 14-year-old female and a 16-year-old female. Both were located, arrested, and charged in connection with the incident. They were later released to a parent on Undertakings and are scheduled to appear in court on July 7
th
, 2026. Emergency Medical Services attended to the victim at the scene and transported her to hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
Failure to Comply Probation x3
On April 27
th
at 11:01 p.m., police received a call notifying of a male and female fighting both inside and outside of an apartment in the 700 block of 6th Street.
Upon arrival, officers determined that a male was inside the residence despite being on conditions prohibiting him from attending the address or communicating with the homeowner. The 30-year-old male was located hiding in a bathroom. He was arrested and transported to BPS detention cells. He is scheduled to appear in court on the morning of April 28
th
, 2026.
Assault with Weapon / Assault
On April 28
th
, just after midnight, police were dispatched to a residence on Alaska Bay for a report of a physical altercation between two individuals inside one of the units. Upon arrival, officers determined that both parties had assaulted one another. During the incident, the 27-year-old female struck the 28-year-old male with a PlayStation 5. Both individuals sustained lacerations to the back of their heads. Both were arrested at the scene. The male will be released on an Undertaking once sober. The female was held in custody and is scheduled to appear in court on the morning of April 28
th
, 2026.
Operate Conveyance while Prohibited
On April 28
th
at 2:44 a.m. a traffic stop was conducted in the 400 block of 5
th
Street. The 27-year-old male operator of the vehicle was found to be prohibited from driving in Canada. Additional tickets were given for driving a vehicle not equipped with ignition interlock and driving an unregistered vehicle. The male was charged and later released via Appearance Notice with a future court date of July 2
nd
, 2026. The vehicle was towed.

title: Police News Release - April 24th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - April 24th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 24, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Theft Under $5000
On April 23rd at 7:53 a.m. Police responded to a request from a store in the 900 block of Victoria Avenue regarding a known to them shoplifter who was in their store while banned. The male had just left without paying for meat. Police located and arrested the 43-year-old male suspect in the 600 block of Rosser Avenue. He was identified via security footage from the store. The male was later released via Appearance Notice with a future court date of July 2
nd
, 2026.
Protective Detention and Care of Intoxicated Persons Act / Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours one individual was arrested for PDCIPA and one for Breach of the Peace. Both individuals were highly intoxicated. They were brought t o Brandon Police Service (BPS) detention cells and will be released once calm/sober.
Arrest Warrants
Over the past 24 hours, five individuals were arrested on outstanding warrants. Two were held in custody for a morning court appearance, two were released on an Appearance Notice, and one was released on an Undertaking with future court dates.
Failure to Attend Court / New Charges
At approximately 1:36 p.m., Police were dispatched to a report of a domestic assault in the area of the 2000 block of McTavish Avenue. A 41-year-old female attended a neighbour’s residence, and it was reported that a known to her 40-year-old male had forcibly kicked in the door to her home nearby while he was possibly armed with a knife. The female was said to have visible minor injuries from him assaulting her such as scratches on her arms and a cut lip. Police responded and confirmed the injuries and noted swelling under her eye as well. The female was intoxicated and not cooperative with Police. It was found that she had an outstanding Winnipeg Police Service (WPS) Warrant for Arrest for Failure to Attend Court. The female was taken into custody on the strength of the WPS Unendorsed Arrest Warrant without issue. She will attend court in the morning on April 24
th
, 2026.
The male suspect had fled the scene but was subsequently located at 2:30 p.m. in the 800 block of Princess Avenue. When Police attempted the arrest of the accused, he fled on foot initiating a brief foot pursuit. He was apprehended a short distance away. At the time of his arrest, he was found in possession of two X-Acto knives, in violation of the conditions of his Probation Order. Both individuals were determined to have been consuming alcohol. The male was also held in custody at BPS for court in the morning of April 24, 2026. The male faces charges of Assault, Resist Arrest, and Failure to Comply Probation.

title: Police News Release - April 23rd, 2026
content:
Police News Release - April 23rd, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 23, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Theft Under $5000
On April 20
th
a male attended a store in the 3400 block of Victoria Avenue and stole approximately $80 worth of meat. He concealed it in his jacket before leaving the store. Video surveillance helped identify the individual from previous occurrences. On April 22
nd
he was located by Police, arrested, and later released via Under with a future court date of July 2
nd
, 2026.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours two individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. Both were held for court.
Disobey Court Order x2/ Criminal Harassment
On April 22
nd
at 7:21 a.m. Police attended a residence in the 400 block of 22
nd
Street to remove a male who was on court orders not to attend the residence or have contact with his ex that lives there. It was learned that the accused has been constantly attempting to communicate with her and has attended the residence multiple times. The 37-year-old male had left prior to Police arrival but was soon located at his residence where he was arrested. He was later released on an Appearance Notice with a court date of June 4
th
, 2026.
Endorsed Warrant for Arrest / New Charges
On April 22
nd
just after noon, Police were called to a business in the 4800 block of Victoria Avenue East after three men were reported on the property, one of whom was believed to be a suspect in an earlier break and enter. Two of the men left the scene in a taxi but were quickly stopped by police and identified, while the third man was said to have left on foot westbound. At approximately 12:36 p.m., officers spotted the suspect who then headed into a tree-lined area near the river, prompting police to establish a perimeter and deploy the K9 unit. Following an extensive search in semi-flooded conditions, the 48-year-old suspect was located but refused to surrender; he was ultimately apprehended, with the suspect, officer, and police dog all becoming soaked during the arrest. A stolen toolbox was recovered in the area and linked to both the suspect and the earlier break and enter. The man was taken to hospital for medical clearance. He was later arrested and held in custody to appear in court on the morning of April 23
rd
, 2026.

Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Failure to Comply Release Order x3
On April 20
th
at 11:00 p.m. a pedestrian stop was conducted in the 900 block of Rosser Avenue. The 24-year-old female was found to be breaching three of the conditions of her Release Order (Curfew, Possession of a Weapon, and Reside Clause). The female was arrested, brought to Brandon Police Service (BPS) detention cells and will attend court in the morning on April 21
st
, 2026.

In the past 24 hours four individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. Three individuals were brought to BPS detention cells and will attend court in the morning on April 21
st
, 2026. One was released with a future court date of May 21
st
, 2026.
Break & Enters

th
at 8:13 a.m. police received a report that sometime between Friday, April 17
th
4:30 p.m. and Monday, April 20
th
at 8:00 a.m., someone had entered a business located in the 900 block of 18
th
Street and stole cash. This incident remains under investigation.
On April 20
th
at 9:06 a.m. police received a report that sometime between Friday, April 17
th
and Monday, April 20
th
at 6:00 a.m. someone entered a construction site in the 100 block of 26
th
Street North and broke into a trailer stealing items from within. This incident remains under investigation.
On April 20
th
at 10:34 a.m. police received a report that sometime over the weekend someone broke into a business in the 2600 block of McDonald Avenue and stool tools valued around $5000. This incident remains under investigation.

th

Police located and apprehended the suspect shortly afterward at a second store. The 43-year-old male was arrested and charged in connection with the theft of approximately $180 worth of goods from the initial incident. He was also charged for a prior theft at a grocery store in the Corral Centre, where he stole $220 worth of meat. Further investigation revealed the male was in breach of existing Undertaking and Probation Orders. He was arrested and taken into BPS detention cells to sober up and prevent further incidents. Once sober, he will be released on an Undertaking with a scheduled court date of June 11
th

Security advised they do not wish to charge for the spitting. Officers attended and located the female who was arrested for Mischief to Property Under $5000. The 20-year-old female was brought to BPS for processing to be released later via Appearance Notice with a future court date of June 15
th
, 2026.
Breach of the Peace
On April 20
th
at 4:50 p.m. police were called to a residence in the 600 block of Princess Avenue regarding a disturbance where two sisters were fighting. A 20-year-old intoxicated female was found to be the primary cause of the issues and was arrested for Breach of the Peace. She was brought to BPS detention cells to be released once sober.

title: Police News Release - April 19th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - April 19th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 19, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
April 19
th
, 2026 -
News Release.
Arrest Warrant
On April 18
th
at 1:36 p.m. during an unrelated traffic stop, officers located a 55-year-old male who was wanted on an outstanding unendorsed Warrant for Arrest for failing to attend court.  He was arrested and brought to BPS detention cells to be held for court the following morning.
Assault
On April 18
th
, just after 3 p.m. police were called to a business in the 800 block of 18
th
St North for fight in the parking lot. Officers attended and learned the fight was between a male and a female, however the male had just left in cab.  Police tracked down the cab, and the 27-year-old male was arrested for assaulting his girlfriend. He was brought to BPS detention cells to be held for court the following morning.
Arrest Warrant
On April 18
th
around 5:15 p.m. police checked on a male in the 1200 block of Princess Ave, who was found to be wanted on an unendorsed Warrant for Theft Under $5000.  The 51-year-old male was arrested and brought to BPS detention cells to be held for court the following morning.
Breach of Court Order
On April 18
th
at approx. 6 p.m. police were dispatched to a residence on Cornell Bay for a disturbance with intoxicated persons. Officers located a 36-year-old female who is on a court order with the condition not to consume alcohol. She was arrested, lodged in BPS cells until sober, and later released on an Appearance Notice with a future court date of June 26
th
, 2026.
Breach of Probation Order X4
On April 18
th
at approx. 7 p.m. police were dispatched to the 800 Block of Victoria Ave for a male acting strange with a utility knife.  Officers located the male, who was known to police, and found with two utility knives, a razor blade and multi-tool knife. The 32-year-old-male was arrested for 4 counts of breaching his probation orders for possessing weapons or knives. He was brought to BPS detention cells to be held for court the following morning.
Drug & Weapons charges
On April 18th, just after 9 pm, Police conducted a traffic stop in the 2100 block of Victoria Avenue with three male occupants. Police observed a white powdery substance in the driver’s door, and arrested all 3 males for Possession of a controlled substance.  During the search of the accused, police located 13 suspected pills of MDMA on the driver, as well as a few grams of Cocaine on the rear seat passenger. The front passenger was found in possession of a replica Glock pistol and plastic “brass” knuckles. All three males were brought to BPS detention cells for processing and later released with conditions to attend court in June.
Commercial Break & Enters
Between 11pm on April 18
th
and 3am on April 19
th
police were alerted to three Break & Enters to businesses in Brandon.  The first was in the 2900 block of Victoria Ave where the suspect rummaged through multiple offices. The 2
nd
was to a business in the 1300 block of 18
th
St, where the suspect was caught on video, and the 3
rd
B&E was to a convenience store in the 1500 block of Richmond where the front glass was smashed and the suspect made off with cigarettes and lottery tickets.   Thanks to the video surveillance from the 1
st
and 2
nd
B&E, which provided a description of the suspect, police were able to use their Remotely Piloted Aircraft as well as K-9 tracking to locate the male suspect responsible for all three incidents.  The 40 year old male was arrested for multiple offences and held in BPS detention cells for Court, where he was remanded to appear on Monday April 20
th
.
Protective Detention + Care of Intoxicated Persons Act
In the last 24 hours, BPS arrested two males in separate incidents for being intoxicated with nowhere else to go.  They were brought to BPS detention cells and will be released when sober.
Breach of Peace
In the last 24 hours, BPS arrested one female and two males from separate incidents who were causing disturbances and were highly intoxicated.  They were arrested for Breach of Peace and brought to BPS detention cells, to be released when sober.
Sgt P. McBeth
Brandon Police Service

content:
Police News Release - April 18th, 2026
Last 24 hours
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 18, 2026
Police Media Releases
Breach of Probation
On
Friday
, April 17
th
around 10:15pm, Police were at a residence in the 2000 block of McDonald Avenue and located a female youth who was bound by a Probation Order not to have contact with the individual she was with. As a result, the 15year-old was charged with Failing to Comply with her Probation Order and was released on an Appearance Notice with a Court date in June.
Firearms Charges
Just after midnight on Saturday April 18
th
, Police attended a residence on Riverheights Drive for an unrelated matter. While in the residence Police observed a long gun lying on the bed, and 3 others on the floor. As a result, a 25year-old female and a 27year-old male were arrested and charged with a number of offences including, Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm and Storage of a Firearm Contrary to Regulations. They were both held in the BPS cells to appear in Court in the morning.
Warrants Executed
Just after 3pm on Friday afternoon, Police located a 46year-old male who was known to have 2 outstanding Warrants for his Arrest. He was arrested and lodged in the BPS cells to appear in Court in the morning.
At approximately 10pm, Police came into contact with a 15year-old male and it was learned that he had a Warrant for his arrest for Failing to Comply with his Probation Order. Due to his level of intoxication, he was held in the BPS cells and released on an Appearance Notice and will appear in Court in May.
Around 11:15pm Police located a female at a business in the 700 block of 10
th
St. and it was confirmed that she had a Warrant of Arrest for Failing to Attend Court. The 30year-old was lodged in cells to appear in Court in the morning.
Breach of Peace
In the last 24 hours 3 people were lodged in the Brandon Police Service for Breach of the Peace related incidents.
S.Sgt. Jeff Hoad

-
Apr 17, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours six individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. Three were held in custody to attend court in the morning, two were released via Appearance Notice and one via Undertaking with future court dates.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours three individuals were arrested for Breach of the Peace. They were brought to Brandon Police Service (BPS) detention cells and will be released once sober/calm.
Theft Under $5000
On April 16
th
, officers located and identified a 27-year-old male suspect wanted in connection with a theft that occurred on April 13
th

th

On April 16
th
police attended a residence on Queens Crescent for a report of a fight. A 24-year-old female was found to be the cause of the disturbance as she was intoxicated and attempting to fight with family. th

title: Police News Release - April 16th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - April 16th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 16, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Arrest Warrants
Over the past 24 hours, five individuals were arrested on outstanding warrants. Of those, three were held for court on the morning of April 16
th
, 2026, while two were released on Appearance Notices with future court dates.
One of the individuals, a 27-year-old female wanted for two counts of Failure to Attend Court and Breach of Probation, resisted arrest. Upon being taken into custody, officers located a multi-tool knife on her person. While undergoing medical clearance, the female spat on an officer. She was subsequently taken to Brandon Correctional Centre (BCC) and incurred three additional charges: Resist Arrest, Possession of a Weapon, and Assaulting a Peace Officer. She will attend court in the morning on April 16
th
, 2026.
Theft Under $5000
On April 15
th
a BPS officer attended BCC and arrested a 20-year-old female in custody on two Theft Under $5000 charges. (One from a local clothing store and one from a convenient store). The female will appear in court in the morning on April 16
th
, 2026.
Theft Under $5000
During a traffic stop on April 15
th
a suspect was identified from two separate thefts from a local grocery store. (April 10
th
for $335 and April 12
th
for $385). The female was arrested and later released via Undertaking with a future court date of June 25
th
, 2026.
Stolen Vehicle Recovered
Police received a call notifying of a vehicle that appeared to be abandoned and left on grass on Agnew Drive. The vehicle was on file as stolen from a Souris RCMP file. The vehicle was towed to BPS for ident and RCMP were notified.
Protective Detention & Care of Intoxicated Persons Act (PDCIPA)
In the past 24 hours two individuals were arrested for PDCIPA. They were brought to BPS detention cells and will be released once sober.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours two individuals were arrested for Breach of the Peace. They were brought to BPS detention cells and will be released once sober/calm.
Stolen Vehicle Leads to Joint Operation and Four Arrested
At approximately 9:51 p.m., members of the Brandon Police Service (BPS) were advised by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) of a vehicle recently stolen from Rivers and believed to be travelling toward Brandon. BPS, Virden and Minnedosa agencies were BOLO’d. (Be on the lookout). BPS members proactively established containment positions along multiple entry routes into the city; however, the suspect vehicle was not located.
At approximately 10:48 p.m., a BPS officer observed the suspect vehicle travelling eastbound on Pacific Avenue. A traffic stop was initiated, but the vehicle failed to stop, proceeding through a stop sign at 8th Street before turning northbound onto 18th Street. Additional BPS units coordinated to maintain controlled surveillance and the potential deployment of tire deflation devices. The suspect vehicle turned westbound onto the Trans-Canada Highway. A second attempt to stop the vehicle was made by two BPS cruisers using emergency lights; however, the driver failed to stop and continued westbound. BPS units disengaged once RCMP units took control beyond city limits.
At approximately 11:02 p.m., RCMP advised that the suspect vehicle was in the area of Kemnay. BPS units coordinated to head to all major junctions on the west side of Brandon. Shortly thereafter, it was confirmed the vehicle had been abandoned in/near Kemnay.
Additional BPS units were authorized to assist including BPS Police Service Dog Unit (K9) and RPAS (Remotely Piloted Aircraft System. AKA - drones). Virden RCMP were also enroute to assist with drone coverage. BPS units, aware of the situation, quickly deployed to assist the RCMP in a coordinated ground and aerial search.
At 11:41 p.m., upon a K9 track and drone assistance, a 29-year-old male was located and apprehended by BPS K9 handler and K9 Aries.
At 12:02 a.m., three additional suspects were tracked and located by K9 Aries and his handler along with the assistance of the BPS drone operator. The three additional suspects, who had attempted to hide between abandoned cars in a bush, were arrested and taken into custody without incident. Two individuals, known to BPS officers, were identified immediately, while the third was identified later. The suspects were a 29-year-old female, an 18-year-old male, and a 36-year-old male.
Post arrest, K9 Aries was further deployed for an article search of the hiding location. No evidence located. Further tracks were attempted with negative results, indicating all suspects were believed to be in custody.
All three remaining suspects were turned over to RCMP shortly thereafter, who have conduct over the investigation.
Through effective inter-agency coordination and the highly trained deployment of the K9, RPAS units, and trained officers - all four suspects were located and arrested without further incident. This outcome reflects the preparedness, discipline, and precision of officers and agencies working collaboratively under unique conditions.
Gun Shot Through Neighboring House
At approximately 5:10 a.m. on April 16
th
, Police were dispatched to the 1300 block of 5th Street following a report of gunshots and a suspected bullet hole in a residence. Officers attended and assessed the direction of the damage. Loud crashing noises were heard from a nearby residence, which was identified as the suspected source.
Police established a perimeter and called for occupants to exit. A male subsequently came to the front door, behaving erratically and failing to comply with police directions. He was safely apprehended and arrested.
An RPAS unit was deployed to clear the residence by drone, followed by officers. No additional individuals were located inside. A rifle was located within the home.
The 29-year-old male remains in custody, and the investigation is ongoing.
J. Reichert

title: Police News Release - April 14th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - April 14th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Apr 14, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours four individuals were arrested for Breach of the Peace. They will be released in the morning once calm/sober.
Arrest Warrants
Over the past 24 hours, five individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. One individual was held in custody for a court appearance in the morning. Three others were released on an Appearance Notice/Undertaking with scheduled future court dates. The fifth individual had his warrant executed and was transported to Brandon Correctional Centre (BCC) due to a Parole Revocation.
Immediate Roadside Prohibition
On April 14
th
at 1:39 a.m. a traffic stop was initiated near Mockingbird and Braecrest Drive. The driver was identified by photo ID and proceeded to fail the ASD (Approved Screening Device) twice. The 23-year-old female was issued an Immediate Roadside Prohibition notice with a 90-day drivers licence suspension and 30-day vehicle seizure. The vehicle was towed.
Dangerous Operation of Motor Vehicle
On February 28
th
, 2026, a BPS officer observed a vehicle on Willowdale Crescent reversing rapidly from a business parking stall with a female hanging out from the front passenger side door. The female was left in the parking lot and the vehicle fled northbound on 26th Street. The vehicle failed to regard red lights, stop signs, police emergency lights and drove on the wrong side of a divided road. Surveillance footage was obtained, and the suspect was later identified.
On April 13
th
, 2026, the 19-year-old male was located in custody at Brandon Correctional Centre and charged. He is scheduled to attend court this morning on April 14
th
,2026.
Break & Enter
On April 13
th
police received a report that suspects had entered a compound in the 2200 block of Park Avenue sometime over the weekend and stole keys to off-road vehicles.
Anyone with information regarding this incident is asked to contact Brandon Police Service at 729-2345 or Crime Stoppers at 727-TIPS (8477). Tips can be provided anonymously, and you may be eligible for a cash reward, also collected anonymously.
Assault & Weapons Incident
At 2:29 p.m. on April 13
th
, a 68-year-old male resident reported that an unknown female approached him in the back lane of Hill Avenue, yelling and screaming, before lunging at him while armed with a knife. The male was able to evade the female.
Further investigation revealed that a second victim had been assaulted by the same suspect in the 1200 block of 5th Street, resulting in a laceration to her arm. The 30-year-old victim was transported to hospital for care by a family member. A third person reported being threatened by the suspect in the 400 block of Brandon Avenue.
Police quickly located the accused in the 400 block of College Avenue, where the 20-year-old female was taken into custody without incident. She was still in possession of the knife at the time of arrest. The female was bound by an Undertaking with a condition not to possess any weapons.
While in custody at Brandon Police Service detention cells, the accused damaged a detention-issued iPhone during a call with her lawyer. She is facing charges of Possession of a Weapon, Assault with a Weapon (x2), Uttering Threats, Failure to Comply with an Undertaking, and Mischief Under $5,000.

Street North reported that there was a male actively trying to gain entry to the business by using tools to force the locked door at the back of the business. Police attended and located a 40-year-old-male on scene. Further investigation revealed that the male had also attempted to break into two other locked businesses in the area. The male was arrested and transported to Brandon Police Service detention cells, where he will be released via an Undertaking once sober. A court date has been set for June 15
th
, 2026.

title: Police Special Media Release
content:
Police Special Media Release
Break and Enter/Theft of Firearms
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Mar 30, 2026
Police Media Releases
Special Media Release
On March 27, 2026, at 10:15 AM, a BPS member attempted to conduct a traffic stop on a truck in the 1000 block of 26
th
Street and the vehicle fled from the officer westbound on Willowdale Crescent, and eventually on to 34
th
Street. Due to the amount of vehicle traffic at the time, the officer discontinued the traffic stop and officers searched the area for the truck. The initial investigation revealed that the truck had previously been stolen from a residence in the 400 block of 22
nd
Street and that the residence itself had been broken into. Numerous firearms and other property were found to have been stolen from within the home.
At 11:33AM, officers located the stolen truck, unoccupied in the 2900 block of Rosser Avenue with numerous firearms and other stolen property in the vehicle. The Major Crime Section took carriage of the investigation with assistance from the Patrol Section, Forensic Identification Section, Organized Crime Section, Community Policing Section, and Police Dog Services Unit. Further investigation led to the identification of four suspects from the vehicle, three of whom were responsible for the Break and Enter the residence in the 400 block of 22
nd
Street. All four suspects were located and arrested at various addresses in the City of Brandon.
On March 27
th
and 28
th
, the Major Crime Section executed search warrants at an address on Elvis Crescent and an address in the 1900 block of Queens Avenue, resulting in the recovery of large amounts of stolen property from the Break and Enter.
Kole Walker Blackbird, 28, of Keeseekoowenin was charged with numerous offences under the Criminal Code, including:
Break and Enter to Steal Firearm
Break and Enter and Commit Theft of Motor Vehicle
Break and Enter and Commit Theft Over $5000
Weapons Trafficking x 3
Possession of a Firearm Knowing its Possession is Unauthorized x 8
Careless Storage of a Firearm x 5
Possession of a Firearm Contrary to Prohibition Order x 8
Fail to Comply with Undertaking x 8
Flight from Peace Officer
Blackbird was remanded into custody.
Shyla Jayne McKay, 21, of Brandon was charged with numerous offences under the Criminal Code, including:
Break and Enter to Steal Firearm
Break and Enter and Commit Theft of Motor Vehicle
Break and Enter and Commit Theft Over $5000
Weapons Trafficking x 3
Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm x 8
Careless Storage of a Firearm x 5
Fail to Comply with Release Order
McKay was remanded into custody.
Cashes Jay Alex Ducharme, 22, of Waywayseecappo was charged with numerous offences under the Criminal Code, including:
Break and Enter to Steal Firearm
Break and Enter and Commit Theft Over $5000
Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm x 5
Ducharme was remanded into custody.
Xavierra Star Sunrise Cook, 23, of Brandon was charged with numerous offences under the Criminal Code, including:
Possession of a Firearm Knowing its Possession is Unauthorized x 5
Possession of a Firearm Contrary to a Prohibition Order x 5
Fail to Comply with a Probation Order x 5
Possession of Property Obtained by Crime
Cook was remanded into custody.
The investigation remains ongoing and anyone with information is asked to call the Brandon Police Service Major Crime Section at 204-729-2345 or Brandon Crimestoppers at 204-727-TIPS (8477).
Cst. M. Jefferies
School Resource Officer

title: Police News Release - March 12th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - March 12th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Mar 12, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours three individuals were arrested for Breach of Peace. They were brought to BPS detention cells to be released in the morning.
Tire Vandalism in South Brandon
On the morning of March 11
th
, many residents in the south end of Brandon woke to find the tires of their vehicles had been slashed. At this time, 72 incidents have been reported.
Police are asking anyone who may have video footage (security cameras, dashcams, etc.) from the area to please upload it using the QR code provided. Officers are working diligently to identify those responsible, and the situation remains under investigation.
If you have any information or relevant footage, please submit it to assist the investigation.
Armed Robbery / Possession of a Weapon / Carrying Concealed Weapon
On March 11
th
at 1:57 p.m., a store in the 300 block of 18th Street North reported being robbed at knifepoint. The suspect stole a $10 sandwich. With the assistance of video surveillance and a suspect description, police were able to quickly locate the individual crossing the Thompson Bridge. A knife was found concealed in his jacket pocket.
The 44-year-old male was highly intoxicated at the time. He was arrested and held in BPS detention cells to attend court.
Utter Threats
On March 10
th
at approximately 9:00 a.m., police responded to a place of employment in the 300 block of 9th Street for a report of a disturbance. A male had entered the building and uttered threats.
On March 11
th
at approximately 3:20 p.m., police located the suspect in the 400 block of 9th Street. A persons stop was conducted, and the 33-year-old male was arrested and brought to BPS for processing. He was later released on an Undertaking with a future court date of May 7
th
, 2026.
Arrest Warrants
On March 11
th
two individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. One was held for court while the other remained in custody at Brandon Correctional Centre.
Failure to Comply Release Order x2
On March 12
th
at 1:28 a.m., a vehicle stop was conducted in the 1600 block of 3rd Street. A 35-year-old female within the vehicle was found to be bound by a curfew condition as well as a reside clause for an address where she no longer lives.
The female was arrested and held in BPS detention cells. She will attend court in the morning.
Assault
On March 12
th
at 12:32 a.m., a 33 male attended BPS to report that he had been assaulted by his ex-partner, a 34-year-old female. The female was reported to have punched the male in the face and pulled his beard during an argument.
Police located the female at her residence, where she was arrested and charged accordingly. She was later released on an Undertaking with a court date of May 21
st
, 2026.

Mar 10, 2026

Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours five individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. Four individuals were released via Appearance Notice, and one was held for court in the morning on March 10
th
, 2026.
Failure to Comply Undertaking
On March 10
th
, 2026, at approximately 12:15 a.m., police responded to a disturbance in the 200 block of Douglas Street following a request to remove an unwanted individual. An intoxicated 19-year-old female was identified as the source of the disturbance and was found to be breaching a court-ordered condition not to consume. The female was arrested and transported to Brandon Police Service (BPS) detention cells, where she will be held until sober. She will be released once sober and is scheduled to appear in court on May 21
st
, 2026.
Break & Enter with Intent / Possess Break-in Instruments
Police responded to a report of a break and enter at the Town Centre, located at 800 Rosser Avenue, on March 9
th
at 7:36 a.m. Security reported that two suspects had entered the building the previous evening. Video surveillance showed a male suspect prying open a door with a prybar while accompanied by a female suspect.
Police later located and identified a 35-year-old male and a 32-year-old female in connection with the incident. The male was found in possession of a prybar and other tools.
A 35-year-old male has been charged with Break and Enter with Intent and Possession of Break-In Instruments. He was released on an Undertaking with a court date of May 21, 2026.
A 32-year-old female has been charged with Break and Enter with Intent. She was released on an Undertaking with a court date of May 18, 2026.

title: Police News Release - March 6th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - March 6th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Mar 06, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Arrest Warrants
In the last 24 hours four individuals were arrested on the strength of their Warrants for Arrest. Three were held for court while one was released via Appearance Notice with a future court date.
Motor Vehicle Accident / Immediate Roadside Prohibition
On March 5
th
at 7:33 p.m., a two-vehicle collision was reported in the 1900 block of Princess Avenue. Vehicle #1 struck Vehicle #2, which was legally parked and unoccupied. The operator of Vehicle #1 initially left the scene but later returned and was identified. The driver was administered an ASD, resulting in two “fail” readings. The vehicle operated by Driver #1 was towed and will be seized for 30 days. The driver’s license was suspended for 90 days.
Sexual Assault / Assault with a Weapon / Forcible Confinement
On March 3
rd
, a statement was obtained from a female who reported that she had been held against her will, assaulted, and sexually assaulted between March 1 and March 2. The female received medical care.
On March 5
th
a 32-year-old male was arrested and held in BPS detention cells. He was scheduled to attend court on March 6
th
, 2026.
Breach of the Peace
In the past 24 hours two highly intoxicated individuals were arrested for Breach of the Peace. They were held in BPS detention cells to be released once sober.
Flight from Police / Resist Arrest / Highway Traffic Act Offences
Shortly after midnight on March 6
th
, police observed a vehicle go through a stop sign in the 600 block of Van Horne Avenue. Emergency lights were activated, and officers followed the vehicle for a short period before the vehicle attempted to take a turn and struck a fence, causing damage. The vehicle then came to a stop in the 1300 block of 8th Street.
Three occupants of the vehicle immediately surrendered to police, while a fourth fled the scene on foot. Officers established a perimeter, and the individual was successfully located with the assistance of a K9 track a short time later in the area of 3rd Street and Victoria Avenue.
The vehicle involved was found to be unregistered and was towed from the scene. The 39-year-old male who had fled from police was identified as the driver and was held in custody to appear in court. The remaining occupants were released without charge.
Stolen Vehicle Recovered
On March 5
th
at 3:33 p.m. Portage la Prairie RCMP advised they had recovered the vehicle that had been reported in yesterday's media release as stolen.

, 2026, at approximately 9:12 a.m., police received a report of a theft from a business located in the 2600 block of Victoria Avenue in Brandon. The investigation determined that a female suspect had been stealing merchandise from the business on multiple occasions between December 18
th
, 2025, and March 1
st
, 2026. The total value of the stolen items is estimated to be approximately $500. Video surveillance was obtained and reviewed by police, and the suspect was identified.
On March 3
rd
, 2026, a 60-year-old female was arrested and charged in connection with the investigation. She was later released on an Undertaking and is scheduled to appear in court on May 11
th
, 2026.
Failure to Comply Undertaking x2
On March 2
nd
, 2026, police conducted a curfew check on a 22-year-old male who was found not to be at his residence, contrary to the conditions of his Undertaking.
At approximately 7:58 p.m. on March 3
rd
, officers conducted a traffic stop in the 2400 block of Park Avenue and the male was identified as an occupant of the vehicle. During the interaction, he advised officers that he no longer resides at the address listed on his Undertaking, stating he had been kicked out of the residence. The male was arrested for breaching his release conditions and lodged in BPS detention cells to appear in court on the morning of March 4
th
, 2026.

title: Police News Release-January 29th, 2026
content:
Police News Release-January 29th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Jan 29, 2026
Police Media Releases
Arrests With Charges in the Last 24 Hours
A 28-year-old female was found intoxicated after a report of a disturbance in the 400 block of 10
th
street. The female was on orders to not communicate with an individual that was also present. She was charged accordingly and lodged until sober, when she will be released for a future court date.
A 39-year-old male was found intoxicated in the 000 block of Willowdale Crescent. The male was on orders to abide by a curfew. He was charged accordingly and lodged until sober, when he will be released for a future court date.
A 19-year-old female was located in the 100 block of 4
th
street after Police responded to the residence for a 911 hang-up call. The female was found to have an endorsed warrant of arrest for Breach of Probation. She was arrested and released for a future court date.
Access Child Pornography/Possess Child Pornography
In August 2025, the Brandon Police Service Major Crimes Section received a CYPERTIP regarding suspected access to child pornography from an IP address located within the city. We recognize that news of this nature is deeply troubling for our community, and we want to assure the public that cases involving the exploitation of children are approached with the highest level of care and diligence.
Following a thorough investigation, a search warrant was executed at a residence in the 3800 block of Centennial Boulevard, where an initial review of the devices confirmed the content referenced in the CYBERTIP. All electronic devices were seized, and a 44-year-old male was arrested without incident.
The suspect was transported to the Brandon Police Service. He was later released on an Undertaking with numerous conditions of which he is required to follow. The accused is scheduled to appear in court on April 9
th
, 2026.
This matter remains under investigation. With this set to be before the courts, no further details will be released at this time.
Intoxicated and Released When Sober
A 27-year-old male was found intoxicated after a report of a disturbance at a residence on Braecrest Drive. He was lodged for his safety and will be released when sober.
A 58-year-old male was found intoxicated after Police received a report of a male causing a disturbance at a licensed premise. He was lodged for his safety and will be released when sober.
A 21-year-old female was found in the middle of the street intoxicated and unable to care for herself. She was lodged for her safety and will be released when sober.
A 38-year-old female was found intoxicated after Police responded to a disturbance in the 400 block of 10
th
street. She was lodged for her safety and will be released when sober.
Cst. Meghan Puteran #157

In the past 24 hours three individuals were arrested for Breach of the Peace. They were held in BPS detention cells to be released once sober/calm.
Charges laid from May 2025 incident
Following an investigation into a robbery that occurred on May 18th, 2025, a 17-year-old male has been charged in connection with the incident. The robbery was reported to have occurred in the 1200 block of 10th Street, where a then 23-year-old male victim stated he was robbed at gunpoint. Through the course of the investigation, the youth was identified and is the final suspect to be charged in this matter; two other suspects have previously been arrested. At the time of the offence, the youth was in contact with three individuals, contrary to the conditions of his probation order.
He was already in custody at the Brandon Correctional Centre on an unrelated matter when he was charged with Armed Robbery and Failure to Comply Probation x3. He will appear in court in the morning.

Police Media Releases
While investigating a separate theft at a business in the 900 block of Victoria Ave, an officer observed a male acting suspiciously while in line for checkout. The 54-year-old male fled the business with $450 worth of merchandise. The officer gave chase and caught the male in the parking lot. All merchandise was recovered and the male was charged accordingly. He will appear in court at a later date.
A 34-year-old female attended BPS as she believed she had a warrant. She was found to have an endorsed warrant of arrest for theft under $5000. She was arrested and released for a future court date.
a 15-year-old female was found to be breaching the conditions of her release. She was arrested and lodged to appear in court later today.
A 26-year-old male was arrested for assault and utter threats for an incident involving Intimate Persons Violence. The accused was held for court today.
Several Alcohol Related Arrests
A 14-year-old male was found intoxicated after a disturbance in the 1300 block of 8
th
street. The male was on orders not to consume alcohol. He was charged accordingly, lodged until sober when he will be released for a future court date.

Police initiated a traffic stop in the 400 block of 6

A 37-year-old male was arrested for breaching his undertaking by contacted a person he was not to have communication with. He was released to appear in court at a later date.

title: Police News Release - January 17th, 2026
content:
Police News Release - January 17th, 2026
By
Brandon Police Service
-
Jan 17, 2026
Police Media Releases
Arrest Warrants
On January 16, 2026, shortly before 11:00 pm, police responded to a report of people fighting at a business in the 000 block of 10
th
street.  The two females (36-year-old and 49-year-old) involved were both found to be wanted on outstanding arrest warrants.  A 30-year-old male who was also fighting was lodged until sober and will receive a fine for Disorderly in a Licensed Premise.
Forgery
On January 7, 2026, police received a report of a client who had attempted to forge a medical prescription at a business in the 2400 block of Victoria Ave.
Following an investigation into the 22-year-old female, she was arrested for Forgery and Use a Forged Document on January 16, 2026.  She was released to appear in court on March 30, 2026.
Breach of Recognizance
On January 13, 2026, police received a complaint of a 15-year-old female who was breaching her no contact order by attending the school of another teen.  On January 16, 2026, the suspect was arrested and released to appear in court on February 10, 2026, on a charge of Breach of Recognizance.
IPDA
On January 17, 2026, at approximately 3:00 am, police responded to a report of a 49-year-old intoxicated female knocking on residential doors.  The female had nowhere to go and was from a rural community. She was lodged until sober.
Fail to Comply with Undertaking
On January 17, 2026, shortly before 4:00 am, police responded to a report of a female trying to fight with others in a residence.  Police learned that the 21-year-old female was to abide by a curfew to be at a rural residence, and not to have any communication with the homeowner.
She was lodged to appear in court on a charge of Breach of Undertaking and Breach of Court Order.
Released by: S/Sgt. Kirby Sararas

By
Brandon Police Service
-
Jan 15, 2026
Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Traffic Stop Leads to Charges
On January 14
th
at 10:29 a.m. a traffic stop in the 2700 block of Richmond Avenue resulted in three individuals receiving charges/tickets. Investigation led to locating 4.8 grams of cocaine, liquor containers, and drug paraphernalia. All individuals were arrested and held for processing.
A 21-year-old male was held for court and received charges of Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking (cocaine) and Breach of Release Order (No contact). He will attend court January 15
th
, 2026.
A 34-year-old female was charged with Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking (cocaine) and later released with a future court date of March 12
th
, 2026.
A 44-year-old male was given a ticket under the LGCCA. Released.
Breach of Probations
In the past 24 hours, two individuals were arrested in separate incidents for breaches of probation. The first involved a 23-year-old male who breached a no-contact condition; he was released on an Appearance Notice with a court date of March 9
th
, 2026. The second incident involved a 14-year-old male youth who breached a 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m. curfew; he was charged and released with a court date of March 24
th
, 2026.
Immediate Roadside Prohibition
On January 14
th
at 4:46 p.m., BPS received information regarding two suspected impaired drivers, one operating a vehicle with Manitoba plates and the other with Ontario plates. One of the vehicles had collided with a snowbank in the area of 50th Street and Victoria Avenue. Both drivers were located and displayed visible signs of impairment. They were arrested, and vehicles were seized.
The drivers, both Ontario residents, were held in BPS detention cells until sober and their identities were confirmed. A 38-year-old male was charged with Refusal, and a 34-year-old male received an Immediate Roadside Prohibition.
IPDA (Intoxicated Persons Detention Act)
On January 14
th
at 10:08 p.m., police received reports of an intoxicated female in the 100 block of 16th Street, walking without shoes. The female was located and determined to be unable to care for herself. She was taken to the BPS detention unit to be released once sober.
Assault / Assault with a Weapon
On January 14
th
at 5:40 p.m. police were informed a woman had been assaulted at a residential bay off Pacific Avenue. A 25-year-old male was arrested and later released via Undertaking with a court date of March 26
th
, 2026.
Theft Under $5000
On January 14
th
at 5:40 p.m., while attending an unrelated call, police located a 20-year-old male who was wanted in relation to a previous theft from a store in the 3400 block of Victoria Avenue, involving merchandise valued at approximately $50. The male was arrested and later released on an Appearance Notice with a future court date.

Police Media Releases
Information in our news releases may relate to issues of violence or trauma, which could be distressing for some individuals. Be mindful of your well-being and consider whether or not engaging with this content is appropriate for you.
Failure to Comply Undertaking / Court Order
In the past 24 hours, two individuals were arrested for breach-related offences. A 36-year-old male was arrested and held for court in the morning after being located hiding under blankets as a passenger in a vehicle occupied by a woman with whom he was prohibited from communicating with under the conditions of an undertaking.
In a separate incident, a 37-year-old male was arrested after attending the place of employment of a female he was court-ordered not to contact or attend. He was later released on an Appearance Notice and is scheduled to appear in court on March 12, 2026.
Arrest Warrants
In the past 24 hours four Arrest Warrants were executed.
Accident Investigation
At 4:42 a.m. this morning someone called in reporting that they had come across a truck wrapped around a tree at the corner of 1
st
street in the 1300 block. The driver, 28-year-old male who had left the scene on foot, was located by police around Richmond and 2
nd
Avenue. The male was vomiting and required an ambulance. The matter remains under investigation.
Breach of the Peace
On January 12
th
at 7:55 p.m. police were called to a residence in the 500 block of Percy Street as a 16-year-old female was intoxicated and causing a disturbance. The female was arrested and will be released once sober in the morning.
Immediate Roadside Prohibition
On January 12
th
at 7:01 p.m. police received a complaint of an impaired driver near the 700 block of Princess Avenue that was driving the wrong way. The vehicle was stopped in the 1100 block of Rosser Avenue. An open bottle as well as several cans of alcohol were observed. The driver, a 31-year-old male, failed two roadside breath samples on the Approved Screening Device. The vehicle was towed and will be impounded for 30 days as well as the driver received a 90-day license suspension. Two additional tickets were given in relation to disobeying road rules and the unlawful transportation of liquor.

Stolen Vehicle Recovered
On January 11
th
at approximately 4:45 p.m., a delivery driver reported that his vehicle was stolen after he left it running outside a business in the 300 block of 10th Street. At 7:12 p.m., officers located the vehicle in the 1500 block of McTavish Avenue. The incident remains under investigation.
Assault / Assault by Choking / Uttering Threats
On January 11
th
at 7:44 p.m. police responded to a report of a male hitting a female in a vehicle near the 1400 block of Van Horne. The first witness reports the female victim tried to get out, but the male driver drove away with her still in the vehicle.  A second call was also reported. Officers located the vehicle in the 2400 block of 24
th
Street. The female had visible bruises and a black eye. A 40-year-old male was arrested and later release via Undertaking with a court date of March 23, 2026.
Arrest Warrant
On January 11
th
at 10:29 p.m. a 34-year-old female was arrested in the 2500 block of Pacific Avenue on the strength of three outstanding Warrants for Arrest. She was held in BPS detention cells for court in the morning.
Breach of the Peace / IPDA (Intoxicated Persons Detention Act)
In the past 24 hours two individuals were arrested for Breach of the Peace and one individual was arrested for IPDA. They will be released once sober.
Possession of Meth / Possession of a Weapon / Unstamped Tobacco
